texto: They have been together for so many years that they are no longer like two people but one strange four-legged creature. For her, so much of their marriage is about talk: she likes to talk, he likes to listen. Without him, she has no one to whom she can address her remarks, her observations, her running commentary about life in general.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Maggie O'Farrell: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “They have been together for so many years that they are no longer like two people but one strange…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, marriage, about pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de casamento e reflexao.

texto: We are all, Esme decides, just vessels through which identities pass: we are lent features, gestures, habits, then we hand them on. Nothing is our own. We begin in the world as anagrams of our antecedents.
explicacao: Citação de Maggie O'Farrell sobre temas ligados a desenvolvimento pessoal. “We are all, Esme decides, just vessels through which identities pass: we are lent features, gestu…” articula desenvolvimento-pessoal, history, identity em registro de desenvolvimento pessoal, com relevância para contextos como identity.

texto: All I was aware of was this hole, this gaping hole where my heart should have been. I read somewhere once that your heart is supposed to be the same size as your clenched fist, but this hole felt far bigger. It seemed to expand over my whole upper body and it felt cold, vacant - the cooling wind seemed to cut right through it. I felt frail and insubstantial, as if the wind could have blown me away.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Maggie O'Farrell: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “All I was aware of was this hole, this gaping hole where my heart should have been. I read somewh…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, grief, heartbreak pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e heartbreak.

texto: An English teacher at school once said to her, 'Alice, one thing I hope you never find out is that a broken heart hurts physically.' Nothing she has ever experienced has prepared her for the pain of this. Most of the time her heart feels as though it's waterlogged and her ribcage, her arms, her back, her temples, her legs all ache in a dull, persistent way: but at times like this the incredulity and the appalling irreversibility of what has happened cripple her with a pain so bad she often doesn't speak for days.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Maggie O'Farrell: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “An English teacher at school once said to her, 'Alice, one thing I hope you never find out is tha…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que educacao, grief, heartbreak pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e heartbreak.

texto: If she was liquid, she would drink her; if she was a gas, she would breathe her; if she was a pill, she would down her'; if she was a dress, she would wear her; a plate, she would lick her clean.
